Finance Process Documentation Report

Single-Site Underground Gold Mine — Process Mapping & Automation Readiness Analysis

OrganisationUnderground Gold Mine (~600 employees)
Finance Team~20 staff, central + site-embedded
Interviews Conducted3 (David Motswari, Priya Desai, James Kipchoge)
Process Domains Scoped14 domains across full finance cycle
ERPEllipse (Hexagon) — not current version
Report PurposeAutomation & AI opportunity identification
📄
14
Process Domains Scoped
✅
4
Well / Fully Documented
⚠️
7
Partially / Minimally Covered
🔴
3
Not Yet Covered

Executive Summary

Three finance staff were interviewed covering accounts payable, procurement, cost accounting, management reporting, business partnering, and systems administration. The interviews reveal a function that is operationally effective due to individual competence but structurally fragile due to undocumented knowledge, disconnected systems, and compensating manual controls. Four structural themes dominate the findings:

🔌 Disconnected Systems

Five source systems (Ellipse, MineRP, Deswik, Microster, Aspen) have no live integrations. Excel is the connective layer for all cross-system analysis and allocation.

👤 Key-Person Concentration

Critical operational intelligence, allocation logic, and compensating controls reside in personal spreadsheets, Outlook folders, and physical files — not in shared systems.

🔁 Upstream Error Propagation

Coding errors at requisition stage propagate uncorrected through the full transaction chain, requiring 30–60 recode journals monthly with no feedback loop to originators.

🛠 Embedded Workarounds

Multiple parallel processes run alongside the ERP (paper packs, tracking spreadsheets, WhatsApp screenshots) because system functionality is unreliable or insufficient.

Process Domain Coverage at a Glance

Status of documentation coverage by domain across 3 interviews.

Coverage is strongest in AP, Procurement, and Cost Accounting — areas directly represented by interviewees. Revenue, Tax, and Intercompany remain entirely undocumented and should be prioritised in the next interview round.

Priority Interviews Recommended

1
Margaret — Finance Manager / Controller
Close process, approval flows, full reporting pack, statutory compliance
2
Amara — Management Accountant
Cost allocation spreadsheet, recode journals, operational reporting
3
Payroll Administrator
Labour cost processing — largest cost category with weakest systemic controls
4
Revenue / AR Accountant
Gold doré sales, revenue recognition, refinery settlements — entirely undocumented

David Motswari's dashed connection to Priya Desai represents an informal control — he is frequently the de facto goods receipt confirmer for contractor invoices, a role formally assigned to Themba (Section Engineer).

Systems Landscape

Core ERP
Integrated (manual)
Not integrated
Shadow/Workaround

Ellipse (Hexagon ERP)

Core ERP System

GL, procurement, inventory, maintenance work orders, approval routing. Version not current. Approval notifications unreliable.

James Kipchoge — Owner

Cognos (BI/Reporting)

Manual Export Required

Reporting layer over Ellipse. Standard cost centre reports generated; must be exported to Excel for any analytical work.

James Kipchoge — Owner

MineRP

No Live Integration

Mine technical system — survey data, development metres. Monthly CSV extract to finance. ~1 week lag on survey data.

Survey Dept — Owner

Deswik

No Live Integration

Mine planning and scheduling. Manual reconciliation to finance data required.

Mine Planning — Owner

Microster (T&A)

Weekly Flat-File Extract

Time and attendance. HR department codes (not finance cost centres). Mapping via shared spreadsheet — two versions exist, no authoritative source.

HR Dept — Owner

Aspen (Plant Processing)

No Integration Whatsoever

Plant operations system. No connection to Ellipse. Mining-to-processing cost allocation handled entirely outside ERP by Amara in Excel.

Plant Operations — Owner

Excel

Pervasive Shadow Layer

Primary analytical and connective layer. David's underground tracker (4yr history), Priya's AP log, Amara's allocation model, payroll mapping table, journals, reconciliations.

Multiple owners

Outlook + WhatsApp

De Facto Workflow / Audit Trail

Email-based approvals, contractor daily activity reports (WhatsApp), invoice routing, business case documentation. Approvals stored in personal Outlook folders.

All staff

Word / Paper Files

Unstructured Knowledge Store

Monthly variance commentary (David). Physical lever arch files and paper invoice packs. Not stored in shared systems.

David Motswari

System Integration Map

Showing data flows between systems — solid lines are automated, dashed lines are manual exports/imports.

graph LR MINE["⛏️ MineRP\nSurvey / Technical"] DES["📐 Deswik\nMine Planning"] MIC["🕐 Microster\nTime & Attendance"] ASP["🏭 Aspen\nPlant Processing"] ELL["🗄️ ELLIPSE ERP\n(Core System)"] COG["📊 Cognos\nBI / Reporting"] XL["📋 EXCEL\n(Connective Layer)"] OUT["📧 Outlook\nApprovals / Comms"] style ELL fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,color:#1e293b style XL fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,color:#1e293b style MINE fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#059669,color:#1e293b style DES fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#059669,color:#1e293b style MIC fill:#fdf4ff,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#1e293b style ASP fill:#fff1f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#1e293b style COG fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,color:#1e293b style OUT fill:#f8fafc,stroke:#94a3b8,color:#1e293b MINE -. "Monthly CSV\n(manual)" .-> XL DES -. "Manual\nreconciliation" .-> XL MIC -. "Weekly flat-file\n(manual extract)" .-> XL ASP -. "No integration\nAllocation only" .-> XL XL -. "Manual journal\nimport" .-> ELL ELL --> COG COG -. "Export to\nExcel" .-> XL ELL -. "Unreliable\nnotifications" .-> OUT
Every data flow into the ERP from operational source systems is manual. Excel acts as the integration bus for the entire finance function. This creates version control risk, key-person dependency, and limits real-time visibility.

Process Domain Coverage Assessment

14 Domains
Well documented
Partially documented
Minimally documented
Not covered
Process Domain Coverage Status Key Roles Documented Critical Gaps Priority
Accounts Payable Well Documented Priya (owner), David (verification), Margaret (auth) Vendor master maintenance; dispute resolution detail Low
Procurement & PO Management Well Documented Priya (processing), James (system), David (contractor) Vendor selection; contract management; requisition compliance Low
Cost Accounting & Allocation Well Documented James (mechanics), David (operational application) Amara's allocation methodology; driver currency; allocation spreadsheet logic Medium
Business Partnering & Business Cases Partially Documented David (primary), Margaret & Johan (approvers) Formal business case template; Capex committee process; post-implementation review Medium
Management Reporting Partially Documented David (variance commentary), James (extraction) Full reporting pack; board/executive reporting; KPI definitions High
Month-End & Year-End Close Partially Documented David (accruals), Amara (recodes — referenced) Full close checklist; reconciliation procedures; year-end specifics; timeline High
Payroll & Labour Cost Processing Partially Documented James (system observation only) Process operator unknown; payroll calculation; leave/overtime rules; payslip generation High
Budgeting & Forecasting Minimally Documented David (budget references in tracker) Full budget cycle; forecasting process; tools; participants; full timeline High
Fixed Assets & Capital Expenditure Minimally Documented David (business case process only) Asset register; depreciation; transfers; impairment; WIP capitalization High
Inventory & Stores Finance Interface Minimally Documented David (explosives), James (Ellipse has inventory) Stores reconciliation; physical counts; obsolescence; slow-moving stock Medium
Cash Management (site-level) Minimally Documented Priya (payment runs, bank details) Cash forecasting; bank reconciliation; cash position monitoring Medium
Accounts Receivable & Revenue Not Covered None Entire process — gold doré sales, revenue recognition, refinery settlements Critical
Tax & Statutory Compliance Not Covered None Tax returns, statutory reporting, audit coordination Critical
Intercompany & Recharges Not Covered None May not apply to single-site operation — needs confirmation Medium

Coverage Distribution

14 process domains by documentation status

Recommended Interview Priority

Estimated coverage gain per additional interview

Documented Process Flows

5 Key Processes

Select a process to view its flow diagram. Flows are constructed from structured interview data and represent processes as they are performed today, including informal workarounds.

📑 AP Invoice to Payment
⛏️ Development Cost Tracking
👷 Labour Cost to GL
🔄 Recode Journal Workflow
🏗️ Contractor Invoice Verification

Accounts Payable — Invoice to Payment

Trigger: Invoice arrives in shared AP inbox  |  Frequency: Continuous intake; weekly payment run (Thursday)

Owner: Priya Desai Well Documented 50–70 invoices/week
← Scroll horizontally to view full diagram
flowchart TD A([📥 Invoice arrives in\nshared AP inbox]) --> B{Document\ntype?} B -->|Invoice| C{PO number\npresent?} B -->|Statement / Query / Other| D[Route to query folder\nOutlook] C -->|Yes| E[Pull PO in Ellipse\nverify GR status] C -->|No ~35%| F[Search Ellipse by\nvendor & date] F --> G{PO\nfound?} G -->|No| H[Park invoice\nLog in tracking spreadsheet\nEmail department to identify owner] G -->|Yes| E E --> I{Goods receipt\nconfirmed?} I -->|Yes| L I -->|No — Standard| J[Chase operational staff\nby email/phone\nTypical delay: 2–3 days] I -->|No — Contractor| K[Phone David Motswari\nor Themba for confirmation\nDavid may send WhatsApp screenshots] J --> L K --> L L[Three-way match:\nPO vs GR vs Invoice\nEllipse header + manual line check] L --> M{Within\ntolerance?} M -->|No| N[Investigate with\nvendor or requester] N --> L M -->|Yes| O[Code & post invoice\nin Ellipse\nGL account + cost centre] O --> P{ERP approval\nnotification\nreliable?} P -->|Yes| R[ERP approval workflow\nAutomated notification to approver] P -->|No — common| Q[Print paper pack\nPhysically deliver to approver\nor place in tray] Q --> S[Approver signs\npaper pack] R --> S S --> T{Wednesday PM\nPayment run prep} T --> U[Pull payment proposal\nfrom Ellipse] U --> V[Manually verify\nbank details\nExcel vendor list] V --> W[Resolve payment holds] W --> X[Margaret reviews &\nauthorizes batch\nEllipse] X --> Y[Release payment\nSend remittance advices\nVendor-specific formats] Y --> Z([✅ Payment cycle complete]) style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style Z fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style H fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 style D fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 style N fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style Q fill:#fdf4ff,stroke:#7c3aed style K fill:#fdf4ff,stroke:#7c3aed
The AP process contains two critical control vulnerabilities: goods receipt confirmation delays cascade into missed payment runs and vendor strain; and the parallel paper-pack process (step P→Q) exists because the ERP approval notification is unreliable — a system defect that has been escalated previously without resolution.

Development Cost Tracking — Blast to Variance Report

Trigger: Development blast occurs (or fails) on a shift  |  Frequency: Continuous underground; weekly tracker update; monthly variance report

Owner: David Motswari Well Documented ~1 week survey lag
← Scroll horizontally if needed
flowchart TD A([⛏️ Development blast occurs\non shift]) --> B[Underground crew drills,\nloads, fires development end] B --> C[Costs incurred:\nDrilling, explosives, labour, equipment] C --> D[Magazine issues explosives\nCost booked on issue\nin Ellipse — regardless of\nblast outcome] style D fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 B --> E[Survey team measures\nadvance after cleanup\nMineRP records metres] E --> F[Monthly CSV extract\nof survey metres\nSent to David & mine planning] style F fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 D --> G[David: Enter survey metres\ninto Excel tracker manually\nSurvey Reconciliation tab] F --> G G --> H{Costs &\nmetres\nalign?} H -->|Yes| I[Update dashboard\nRAG status &\ncost per metre] H -->|No| J[David investigates:\nChecks survey report\nVisits underground\nSpeaks to shift boss] J --> K{Timing issue\nor real cost\nvariance?} K -->|Timing| L[Phone Margaret\nAgree accrual treatment] K -->|Real variance| M[Document in tracker\nFlag in variance commentary] L --> N[Margaret's team posts\nmanual accrual journal\nin Ellipse\nDebit CC / Credit accrual] N --> I M --> I I --> O[Update tracker dashboard:\nRAG status per development end\n~15% threshold rule — informal] O --> P[Write monthly variance\ncommentary in Word doc\nAll underground cost centres] P --> Q[Email to Margaret & Johan\nFile locally + lever arch file\nNot in shared system] style Q fill:#fdf4ff,stroke:#7c3aed Q --> R([📊 Monthly reporting complete]) style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style R fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style J fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626
The ~1-week survey lag between cost incurrence and metre confirmation creates a structural estimation requirement every month. David's four-input judgment framework (system data, survey, physical inspection, shift boss) is undocumented and entirely dependent on his presence. The 15% RAG threshold rule is informal and not codified anywhere.

Labour Cost Processing — Time & Attendance to General Ledger

Trigger: Weekly T&A extract from Microster  |  Frequency: Weekly extract; monthly reconciliation

Process Operator Unknown Partially Documented Weakest systemic controls
flowchart TD A([⏱️ Weekly extract triggered\nfrom Microster]) --> B[HR / T&A system generates\nflat-file: Employee ID,\nhours, shift type,\nHR department code] B --> C[Finance receives extract\nProcess operator UNKNOWN] style C fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 C --> D[Apply HR-to-Finance\ncost centre mapping table\nin Excel spreadsheet] style D fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 D --> E{All HR codes\nhave valid\nmapping?} E -->|Yes| F[Manual review\nfor anomalies\nExtent of review unknown] E -->|No — new/changed code| G[Manual investigation\nby finance staff] G --> F F --> H[Import or manually journal\nlabour costs into Ellipse\nby cost centre] style H fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 H --> I{Import\nvalidation\ncheck?} I -->|None exists| J[Incorrect mappings post\nsilently to wrong\ncost centre] style J fill:#dc2626,color:#fff I -->|N/A| K[Labour costs in Ellipse\nledger — may be incorrect] J --> K K --> L[Month-end: Amara reviews\ncost centre reports\nPattern recognition only] L --> M{Anomaly\ndetected?} M -->|Yes| N[Recode journal raised\nSee Recode Journal flow] M -->|No — common| O[Error persists in ledger] style O fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 N --> P([✅ Corrected labour costs\nin management accounts]) O --> P style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style P fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669
Labour is the largest single cost category and has the weakest systemic controls. The dual-version mapping table (HR and Finance maintain separate copies with no authoritative source or change protocol) means any org restructure can silently corrupt labour cost allocations. The absence of import validation means errors are only detected by human pattern recognition — if at all.

Month-End Recode Journal Workflow

Trigger: Amara identifies miscoded transactions during cost report review  |  Frequency: Monthly — 30–60 journals estimated

Owner: Amara (Management Accountant) Well Documented 30–60 per month
flowchart TD A([📊 Month-end cost report\nreview begins]) --> B[Amara exports cost centre\nreports from Ellipse/Cognos\nto Excel] B --> C[Review line items by\ncost centre for anomalies\nPattern recognition only] C --> D{Anomalies\nidentified?} D -->|No| Z([✅ Reports accepted\nas-is]) D -->|Yes| E[Investigate source transaction\nin Ellipse\nDrill into PO, work order,\noriginal requisition] E --> F{Miscoding\nconfirmed?} F -->|No| G[Accept coding\nNote for context] G --> C F -->|Yes| H[Identify correct\ncost centre / account code] H --> I{Additional\ncontext\nneeded?} I -->|Yes| J[Chase originator by\nemail or conversation] J --> K[Originator confirms\ncorrect coding] I -->|No| L K --> L L[Prepare recode journal\nin Excel template\nDebit: correct CC\nCredit: original CC] L --> M[Enter journal into Ellipse\nFree-text narrative field\ninconsistently populated] style M fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 M --> N{Amount above\napproval\nthreshold?} N -->|Yes| O[Margaret reviews\nand approves journal\nin Ellipse] N -->|No| P[Journal posted\nto ledger] O --> P P --> Q{Feedback to\noriginator?} Q -->|None sent — gap| R[Originator not informed\nSame error likely to recur\nnext month] style R fill:#dc2626,color:#fff style Q fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 P --> S([✅ Ledger corrected\nfor this month]) style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style S fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669
The recode journal process fixes symptoms but not causes. The absence of any feedback loop to originators means the same upstream coding errors recur monthly, generating a predictable workload of 30–60 journals. The inconsistently populated
